issues.description: 原文的 'share a 14nm slot' 是一种宽泛的说法（指代同一代际的代工位置），但台积电当时为苹果代工 A9 芯片实际使用的是 16nm 工艺，三星使用的是 14nm 工艺。译文直译为“瓜分 14 纳米订单”会给半导体专业读者造成“台积电当时也生产了 14nm 芯片”的技术误解。
“filled... capacity” 在制造业和代工语境下指“填满/占据/消化产能”，译文使用“填补”不够准确。“填补”通常指弥补空缺或亏空（如填补空白、填补亏损），在此处搭配略显生硬。

paragraphs.source: First, Apple’s key supplier Samsung entered smartphones 18 months after iPhone launched, ending up in a legal battle with Apple as early Galaxy S designs resembled iPhones. The unease grew with each Samsung’s release and its growing competitive position smartphones, pushing Apple to look for alternatives.

pro.text: 第一，苹果的核心供应商三星在 iPhone 发布 18 个月后进军智能手机市场，由于早期的 Galaxy S 设计酷似 iPhone，双方最终陷入法律纠纷。随着三星每次发布新机及其在智能手机领域的竞争地位不断攀升，苹果的不安感与日俱增，迫使其寻找替代方案。

paragraphs.source: Second, the rise of the Wintel model (Android plus Qualcomm) in smartphones in the 2010s worried Apple that commodity chips could erode its differentiation in software and affect its premium status. Jobs made the call in 2008: Apple would design its own chips. But manufacturing requires 10s of billions in fabs. So Apple adopted a fabless systems company approach.

Jobs made the call in 2008: Apple would design its own chips. But manufacturing requires 10s of billions in fabs. So Apple adopted a fabless systems company approach.

pro.text: 第二，2010 年代智能手机领域“Wintel”模式（安卓加高通）的崛起让苹果感到担忧，害怕通用芯片会削弱其在软件方面的差异化优势，并影响其高端定位。乔布斯在 2008 年拍板决定：苹果将自研芯片。但制造芯片需要耗资数百亿美元建设晶圆厂。因此，苹果采取了无晶圆厂（fabless）系统公司模式。

paragraphs.source: Apple wanted to control the primary technologies used in its devices. The $278M P.A. Semi acquisition in April 2008 served as a stepping stone. Dan Dobberpuhl, creator of Alpha and StrongARM, had assembled 150 of the best low-power chip engineers in the world. Among them was Johny Srouji, an Israeli engineer from Intel and IBM. He now runs Apple Silicon as SVP of Hardware Technologies.

paragraphs.source: Enter Intel and TSMC, two of the leading fab options for Apple. Discussions with Intel were unsuccessful as then CEO Paul Otellini declined, believing the volume wouldn’t justify the low margin and rigorous demands Apple placed on suppliers. At TSMC, Morris Chang accepted the challenge, viewing it as a growth opportunity rather than a margin drag.

paragraphs.source: Apple COO Jeff Williams met with Chang over dinner and pitched TSMC to build 20nm capacity. At that time, TSMC was shifting focus and investment to 16nm. The capital and capacity numbers Apple asked were unheard of; they went so far as suggesting TSMC cut its dividend to fund the fab buildout. TSMC made the bet. They were able to fund the fabs with debt. At the time of the initial decision, success was far from a sure thing for either side.

paragraphs.source: Apple’s A8 chip launched in 2014, and TSMC never looked back. Over the next six years, Apple drove TSMC to invest $60-80 billion in leading-edge capacity. Apple’s volume justified every major node transition: N16, N7, N5. Without the iPhone’s annual 200M unit baseline, TSMC could not have afforded the R&D velocity that left Intel and Samsung behind.

paragraphs.source: At the core there are cultural differences: Intel, trapped in its product success, couldn’t foresee the massive Arm-based foundry market; TSMC offered servitude, flexibility, and a willingness to ‘bet the company’ on Apple’s success. TSMC built dedicated capacity, accepted Apple’s engineers into their labs, and aligned their roadmap with the iPhone’s annual cycle. This cultural alignment, TSMC’s willingness to customize versus Intel’s standardized product approach, remains Intel’s biggest hurdle in its IDM 2.0 pivot today.

TSMC built dedicated capacity, accepted Apple’s engineers into their labs, and aligned their roadmap with the iPhone’s annual cycle. This cultural alignment, TSMC’s willingness to customize versus Intel’s standardized product approach, remains Intel’s biggest hurdle in its IDM 2.0 pivot today.

pro.text: 核心在于文化差异：英特尔受困于自身产品的成功光环，未能预见基于 Arm 架构的庞大晶圆代工市场；台积电则展现出极致的服务姿态与灵活性，甚至拿“全公司的命运”押注苹果的成功。台积电为苹果建设了专属产能，允许苹果工程师进驻其实验室，并将自身的技术路线图与 iPhone 的年度迭代周期深度绑定。这种文化上的契合度，体现为台积电甘愿定制的姿态与英特尔死守标准化产品的模式截然不同。时至今日，这依然是英特尔向 IDM 2.0 转型过程中的最大障碍。

paragraphs.source: Despite investing in dedicated 20nm capacity for Apple, TSMC did not even receive a majority share of Apple’s foundry business at first. It had to share a 14nm slot with Samsung in 2015 with the latter getting >60% share. TSMC management was shocked, but responded by accelerating their next-gen 10nm process.

pro.text: 尽管台积电为苹果投资建设了 20 纳米专属产能，起初却连苹果晶圆代工业务的大头都没能拿下。2015 年，台积电不得不与三星瓜分 14 纳米订单，且后者抢走了超过 60% 的份额。台积电管理层大为震惊，但随即予以反击：加速推进下一代 10 纳米工艺。

paragraphs.source: What if Apple chose Intel in 2014? Intel would have $15B+/year guaranteed foundry revenue. TSMC, without that revenue, would probably not have achieved dominance anywhere near what it has today. Intel Foundry would be 10 years more mature. It’s the biggest misstep in the history of chip foundries.

pro.text: 试想，如果苹果在 2014 年选择了英特尔会怎样？英特尔每年将获得超过 150 亿美元的保底晶圆代工营收。失去这笔营收的台积电，恐怕根本无法企及如今的霸主地位。英特尔的晶圆代工业务则会比现在成熟十年。这是芯片代工史上最大的战略失误。

paragraphs.source: By 2020, the partnership had evolved from mutually beneficial to co-dependence. Apple could no longer leave. No other foundry on earth could produce M-series and A-series chips at the required volume and yield. Samsung’s 3nm yields were 30-40% versus TSMC’s 80%+. The switching cost was estimated at $2-5 billion in redesign and requalification alone.

pro.text: 到 2020 年，双方的合作关系已从互利共赢演变为相互依存。苹果再也无法抽身。放眼全球，没有任何一家其他晶圆代工厂能在产能和良率上满足 M系列和 A系列芯片的要求。三星的 3 纳米良率仅为 30% 至 40%，而台积电则高达 80% 以上。仅重新设计和重新认证带来的转换成本，估计就高达 20 亿至 50 亿美元。

issues.description: 原文的 'share a 14nm slot' 是一种宽泛的说法（指代同一代际的代工位置），但台积电当时为苹果代工 A9 芯片实际使用的是 16nm 工艺，三星使用的是 14nm 工艺。译文直译为“瓜分 14 纳米订单”会给半导体专业读者造成“台积电当时也生产了 14nm 芯片”的技术误解。
“filled... capacity” 在制造业和代工语境下指“填满/占据/消化产能”，译文使用“填补”不够准确。“填补”通常指弥补空缺或亏空（如填补空白、填补亏损），在此处搭配略显生硬。
中英文排版规范细节：译文“M系列和 A系列”中，“M”与“系列”之间没有空格，而“A”与前面的“和”之间有空格，排版不够统一。

Moving to Intel or Samsung would mean 2-3 years of inferior products while yield learning catches up. Product upgrade cycles, most importantly the annual iPhone refresh synced with holidays, would also be at risk.

paragraphs.source: For years, Hsinchu and Cupertino worked as a single team to relentlessly advance Moore’s Law. Apple’s One Team approach embedded hundreds of engineers at TSMC’s headquarters, effectively treating the foundry as an extension of Cupertino. This team co-developed Process Design Kits (PDKs), ensuring that when a new node like 5nm launched, Apple’s designs were perfectly synthesized to the transistor characteristics.

This team co-developed Process Design Kits (PDKs), ensuring that when a new node like 5nm launched, Apple’s designs were perfectly synthesized to the transistor characteristics.

pro.text: 多年来，新竹与库比蒂诺宛如一体，不遗余力地推动摩尔定律向前发展。苹果采用“同一团队”（One Team）模式，将数百名工程师派驻台积电总部，实际上已将这家晶圆代工厂视为库比蒂诺的延伸。该团队共同开发工艺设计套件（PDK），确保当 5 纳米等新制程节点发布时，苹果的设计能与晶体管特性完美契合。

paragraphs.source: However, the rise of generative AI is shifting TSMC’s customer mix. While Apple remains the largest single customer by revenue, its relative influence is diluting as the High-Performance Computing (HPC) segment, driven by NVIDIA, AMD, and hyperscalers, outgrows. In Q1 2020, smartphones accounted for 49% of TSMC’s revenue, while HPC was 30%. By Q3 2025, HPC had skyrocketed to 57%, relegating smartphones to a secondary growth driver.

pro.text: 然而，生成式 AI 的崛起正在重塑台积电的客户结构。尽管按营收计算，苹果依然是最大的单一客户，但在英伟达、AMD 以及超大规模云厂商的驱动下，HPC 业务板块的增长势头更为迅猛，苹果的相对影响力正被稀释。2020 年第一季度，智能手机占台积电营收的 49%，而 HPC 占 30%。到了 2025 年第三季度，HPC 占比已飙升至 57%，智能手机退居为次要增长引擎。

paragraphs.source: While Apple also contributes to TSMC’s HPC segment through its tablet/PC chips, the uptake of AI offered TSMC a new stream of customers who are voracious for advanced node capacity. While AI accelerators moved 1 year cadence, they are still on n-1 node largely. Apple will still be the anchor customer on N2 (2nm) but it will see stiff competition from others competing for capacity. On A16 (1.6nm), HPC players are likely to beat Apple as it is a more HPC centric node.

pro.text: 尽管苹果的平板电脑与 PC 芯片同样为台积电的 HPC 业务做出了贡献，但 AI 的爆发为台积电带来了一批对先进制程节点产能如饥似渴的新客户。虽然 AI 加速器已转向一年一更的迭代步调，但它们目前主要仍在使用 n-1 节点。苹果依然会是 N2（2 纳米）的基石客户，但将面临其他争夺产能者的激烈竞争。而在 A16（台积电 1.6 纳米埃米级节点，而非苹果处理器）上，由于该节点更侧重于 HPC，HPC 厂商很可能会击败苹果抢占先机。

paragraphs.source: The distinction lies in what these customers are buying. Apple buys leading-edge logic wafers (N3, N3E) and InFO (Integrated Fan-Out) packaging. paragraphs.source: This creates a bifurcation in TSMC’s capacity planning. Apple is no longer the sole driver of capex. TSMC’s capital expenditure is now split between chasing Moore’s Law (2nm for Apple) and chasing packaging density (CoWoS-L for NVIDIA). Apple acts as the predictable baseline that justifies the massive fixed costs of new fabs, while NVIDIA provides the high-margin upside that drives profitability growth. The power dynamic has shifted from a unipolar world (Apple) to a bipolar world (Apple + AI), where TSMC can now arbitrage demand between the two sectors to maintain pricing power.

pro.text: 这导致台积电的产能规划出现分化。苹果不再是资本支出的唯一驱动力。台积电目前的资本支出被一分为二：一部分用于推进摩尔定律（为苹果研发 2nm），另一部分用于提升封装密度（为英伟达扩充 CoWoS-L）。苹果充当着可预测的业绩底盘，为新建晶圆厂庞大的固定成本提供了合理性支撑。英伟达则带来了高毛利的上升空间，驱动利润率增长。权力格局已从单极世界（苹果）转向双极世界（苹果加 AI），台积电如今可以在两大领域的不同需求之间进行套利，借此维持定价权。

Apple could qualify Intel, initially for lower-risk silicon such as base M-series, and yield well. That would give Intel reference design wins and Apple supply chain diversification without risking core products.

中英文排版规范细节：译文“M系列和 A系列”中，“M”与“系列”之间没有空格，而“A”与前面的“和”之间有空格，排版不够统一。

paragraphs.source: Intel missed Apple once before in 2014. The door hasn’t fully closed. The key question is: would Apple actually use it?

pro.text: 英特尔曾在2014年错失过苹果。如今合作的大门并未完全关闭。核心问题在于：苹果真的会采用吗？

paragraphs.source: But, 18A-P on base M-series chips makes sense. If Apple were to shift 20% of base M-series wafers to Intel 18A-P, it would imply $630M in foundry revenue for Intel at an $18k ASP.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: But, 18A-P on base M-series chips makes sense. If Apple were to shift 20% of base M-series wafers to Intel 18A-P, it would imply $630M in foundry revenue for Intel at an $18k ASP.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: But, 18A-P on base M-series chips makes sense. If Apple were to shift 20% of base M-series wafers to Intel 18A-P, it would imply $630M in foundry revenue for Intel at an $18k ASP.

pro.text: 不过，将18A-P用于基础款M系列芯片确实合乎逻辑。如果苹果将20%的基础款M系列晶圆订单转移给英特尔18A-P，按1.8万美元的平均售价 (ASP) 计算，这将为英特尔带来6.3亿美元的晶圆代工厂营收。

paragraphs.source: While Intel’s current 18A yields are lower than TSMC’s N3 for a 150mm² die (>80%), Intel offers pricing leverage, potential 14A optionality, and US-based wafer/packaging capabilities for Apple.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: While Intel’s current 18A yields are lower than TSMC’s N3 for a 150mm² die (>80%), Intel offers pricing leverage, potential 14A optionality, and US-based wafer/packaging capabilities for Apple.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: While Intel’s current 18A yields are lower than TSMC’s N3 for a 150mm² die (>80%), Intel offers pricing leverage, potential 14A optionality, and US-based wafer/packaging capabilities for Apple.

pro.text: 尽管对于150平方毫米的裸片，目前英特尔18A的良率仍低于台积电N3（大于80%），但英特尔能为苹果提供议价筹码、潜在的14A工艺选择权，以及美国本土的晶圆制造与封装能力。

paragraphs.source: There are a few lower impact possibilities too: while the Intel16 mature node in its Ireland fab could address DTV and connectivity apps, we doubt the capacity is enough to serve Apple. Apple could qualify Intel for lower-risk silicon: WiFi/Bluetooth, display drivers, or power management. That would give Intel reference design wins and Apple supply chain diversification without risking core products.

pro.text: 此外还有一些影响较小的备选方案：虽然英特尔爱尔兰晶圆厂的Intel16成熟制程节点足以应对数字电视 (DTV) 和连接应用，但我们怀疑其产能无法满足苹果的需求。苹果可对英特尔的低风险芯片进行认证，例如WiFi/蓝牙芯片、显示驱动芯片或电源管理芯片。这既能让英特尔获得标杆客户背书，又能让苹果在不危及核心产品的前提下实现供应链多元化。
